entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: One cannot seize the undivided word directly

entities.name: chandanah
entities.cite: the hearer takes part-cognitions to be cognitions of real parts of the word

entities.name: antarāla
entities.cite: In the antarala, the interval before the final ascertainment

entities.name: aśaktiḥ
entities.cite: this, says the verse, is asaktih, an incapacity of the apprehender

entities.name: grahaṇam
entities.cite: that very incapacity is the means of grasping

axioms.name: asata
axioms.cite: the appearance of phonemic parts is itself diagnosed as error — but productive error

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: cognition proceeds through a staged fiction of parts that ripens (1.84) into the true unitary grasp
sa grahanopaya eva sah — that very incapacity is the means of grasping

denials.name: pratibandha
denials.cite: The mistake is not an obstacle the theory must explain away; it is the mechanism

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: the parts of the word exist in the interval
that is the apprehender's incapacity; that, in fact, is the very means of the grasping

rejected_readings.label: obstacle_model
rejected_readings.rendering: The apprehender's incapacity to perceive parts directly is an obstacle to grasping the word.
rejected_readings.why: Treats incapacity as a defect or barrier rather than as the productive mechanism itself. The commentary explicitly inverts this: the incapacity IS the means, not an impediment to it. This denial appears in the axioms.

rejected_readings.label: real_parts
rejected_readings.rendering: The phonemic parts truly exist as distinct entities within the interval before final ascertainment.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts the phonemic parts as real (sat), whereas the verse and commentary insist they are asata (non-existent) — a productive appearance, but not substantial reality. This reverses the sort: chandana would be an absolute rather than a manifestation.

notes: The verse articulates the paradox of staged cognition: the phonemic parts are neither fully real nor mere illusion, but a productive fiction (vivarta) that enables the mind to ascend to unified comprehension. The incapacity-as-means structure parallels the world-as-appearance doctrine (1.86 generalizes this). The contested note in the corpus flags syntactic ambiguity (double sah), but the doctrine — that error is mechanism — is secure.
